# Test Fixtures

Realistic PDFs that embed **real macOS system fonts** (TrueType, not base-14
substitutes) so the in-place text editor can be exercised against documents that
look like the ones a user actually opens. Every fixture is US Letter (612x792
pt), single page, generated by `build_fixtures.py` with PyMuPDF's `insert_text`
+ `fontfile=` so the original face is embedded.

## How to rebuild

```sh
QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen \
  /Users/edward/Documents/GitHub/PDFTextEditor/.venv/bin/python \
  tests/fixtures/build_fixtures.py
```

This regenerates every `*.pdf`, renders a verification `*.png` of each (2x zoom,
1224x1584), and writes `font_reports.json` with the raw per-fixture
`page.get_fonts(full=True)` and `doc.extract_font()` output.

## Source fonts

All from `/System/Library/Fonts/Supplemental/`:

| File | Family / style | Full size on disk |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Arial.ttf` | Arial Regular | 773,236 B |
| `Arial Bold.ttf` | Arial Bold | 750,984 B |
| `Arial Italic.ttf` | Arial Italic | 553,284 B |
| `Times New Roman.ttf` | Times New Roman Regular | 824,920 B |
| `Times New Roman Bold.ttf` | Times New Roman Bold | (used via mix only) |
| `Times New Roman Italic.ttf` | Times New Roman Italic | (available) |
| `Georgia.ttf` | Georgia Regular | 379,588 B |
| `Georgia Bold.ttf` | Georgia Bold | 363,800 B |
| `Georgia Italic.ttf` | Georgia Italic | 400,624 B |
| `Comic Sans MS.ttf` | Comic Sans MS Regular | 135,484 B |
| `Comic Sans MS Bold.ttf` | Comic Sans MS Bold | 120,120 B |

## Reading the font reports

`page.get_fonts(full=True)` returns one tuple per font *reference* on the page:
`(xref, ext, type, basename, refname, encoding)`. PyMuPDF embeds each distinct
face once (one `xref`) but a fresh `refname` is created per `insert_text` call,
so the same face can appear multiple times in the list at the same `xref`. The
manifest tables below are **deduplicated by `xref`** (the real embedded faces).

`doc.extract_font(xref)` returns `(basename, ext, type, bytes)`. A six-letter
prefix on the basename (e.g. `HXRMZO+Comic Sans MS Regular`) marks a subsetted
font. All faces embed as `ttf` / `Type0` with `Identity-H` encoding.

The `font` string the editor sees via `page.get_text("rawdict")` (and the
project's `document.py` `Span.font`) is the PostScript name, which differs from
the `extract_font` basename, e.g. embedded basename `Times New Roman Regular`
surfaces to the span layer as `TimesNewRomanPSMT`.

---

## Fixtures

### 1. `body_paragraphs.pdf` — multi-paragraph body text

Three paragraphs, four real-sentence lines each, single family / size / color.
The clean baseline case for paragraph and line-geometry handling.

- **Embedded faces (by xref):** Times New Roman Regular — `ttf` / `Type0`, 824,920 B, not subset.
- **Span font name:** `TimesNewRomanPSMT`
- **Sizes:** 11 pt
- **Colors:** black `(0, 0, 0)`
- **Subset:** no
- **Span count (page 0):** 12

### 2. `multi_size.pdf` — multiple font sizes on one page

A report header stack: title, subtitle, deck, section line, footnote — one
family, five distinct sizes.

- **Embedded faces (by xref):** Arial Regular — `ttf` / `Type0`, 773,236 B, not subset.
- **Span font name:** `ArialMT`
- **Sizes:** 8, 11, 13, 18, 28 pt
- **Colors:** black `(0, 0, 0)`
- **Subset:** no
- **Span count (page 0):** 5

### 3. `bold_italic.pdf` — bold and italic runs

Regular / bold / italic Georgia on their own lines, plus one line mixing
regular + bold + italic runs side by side.

- **Embedded faces (by xref):**
  - xref 5: Georgia Regular — `ttf` / `Type0`, 379,588 B
  - xref 12: Georgia Bold — `ttf` / `Type0`, 363,800 B
  - xref 19: Georgia Italic — `ttf` / `Type0`, 400,624 B
- **Span font names:** `Georgia`, `Georgia-Bold`, `Georgia-Italic`
- **Sizes:** 15 pt
- **Colors:** black `(0, 0, 0)`
- **Subset:** no
- **Span count (page 0):** 8

### 4. `colored_text.pdf` — colored (non-black) text

Five lines, five non-black colors, across Arial Regular / Bold / Italic.

- **Embedded faces (by xref):**
  - xref 5: Arial Bold — `ttf` / `Type0`, 750,984 B
  - xref 12: Arial Regular — `ttf` / `Type0`, 773,236 B
  - xref 19: Arial Italic — `ttf` / `Type0`, 553,284 B
- **Span font names:** `Arial-BoldMT`, `ArialMT`, `Arial-ItalicMT`
- **Sizes:** 11, 16 pt
- **Colors (RGB 0..1, as the span layer reports them):**
  - navy `(0.10, 0.16, 0.45)`
  - crimson `(0.79, 0.09, 0.13)`
  - forest `(0.13, 0.45, 0.20)`
  - plum `(0.45, 0.16, 0.50)`
  - slate `(0.28, 0.33, 0.40)`
- **Subset:** no
- **Span count (page 0):** 5

### 5. `subset_font.pdf` — SUBSETTED font (missing-glyph fallback) ← the subset case

Three lines of Comic Sans (regular + bold), then `doc.subset_fonts()` is called
before saving so only the glyphs actually drawn survive in the embedded faces.
This is the fixture that exercises the **missing-glyph fallback** path: any
character outside the drawn set is no longer in the embedded font.

- **Embedded faces (by xref), after subsetting:**
  - xref 5: `HXRMZO+Comic Sans MS Regular` — `ttf` / `Type0`, **15,481 B** (down from 135,484 B), subset prefix present.
  - xref 13: `ZKFUMC+Comic Sans MS Bold` — `ttf` / `Type0`, **12,183 B** (down from 120,120 B), subset prefix present.
  - (The six-letter prefix is randomized per build, e.g. `HXRMZO+`, `ZKFUMC+`.)
- **Span font names:** `Comic Sans MS Regular`, `Comic Sans MS Bold`
- **Sizes:** 16 pt
- **Colors:** black `(0, 0, 0)`, navy `(0.10, 0.16, 0.45)`, crimson `(0.79, 0.09, 0.13)`
- **Subset:** **YES** — verified with fontTools: the embedded Regular face has the
  `cmap` table stripped and only **20 of 587** glyphs retain real outlines (the
  other 567 are blanked), so the embedded font can render the page but cannot
  supply glyphs for newly typed characters. File shrank ~88%.
- **Span count (page 0):** 3

### 6. `mixed_families.pdf` — two families on one page

Comic Sans MS headings (bold title, regular subhead and casual note) against a
Times New Roman body paragraph.

- **Embedded faces (by xref):**
  - xref 5: Comic Sans MS Bold — `ttf` / `Type0`, 120,120 B
  - xref 12: Times New Roman Regular — `ttf` / `Type0`, 824,920 B
  - xref 21: Comic Sans MS Regular — `ttf` / `Type0`, 135,484 B
- **Span font names:** `ComicSansMS-Bold`, `TimesNewRomanPSMT`, `ComicSansMS`
- **Sizes:** 12, 16, 22 pt
- **Colors:** black `(0, 0, 0)`, navy `(0.10, 0.16, 0.45)`, crimson `(0.79, 0.09, 0.13)`, slate `(0.28, 0.33, 0.40)`
- **Subset:** no
- **Span count (page 0):** 7

### 7. `form_like.pdf` — synthetic FORM (full-editor feature fixture) ← built by `build_form_fixture.py`

A PII-free form mirroring the visual structure of a background-check / report
form, built to exercise the *full editor* features (select/move/resize/delete
boxes, font family/size/color/bold-italic inspector, add-text on empty canvas).
Every label, value, and date is invented — no real data. Built by
`build_form_fixture.py` (not `build_fixtures.py`).

Layout: a solid blue header bar with WHITE bold title text; a five-row two-column
field table (light-blue-filled bold label cells, white value cells); a large
empty lower band under "Notes:" for placing brand-new text boxes; a gray footer.

- **Header:** solid fill `(0.16, 0.32, 0.55)`, white bold "Sample Report" + a
  pale subtitle, both base-14 Helvetica.
- **Table fills / borders:** light label fill `(0.90, 0.93, 0.97)`, gray grid
  `(0.62, 0.67, 0.74)` — these test that non-destructive redaction
  (`PDF_REDACT_IMAGE_NONE` / `PDF_REDACT_LINE_ART_NONE`) keeps cell fills and
  borders intact when a value is edited.
- **Embedded faces (by xref):**
  - Helvetica-Bold — base-14 `Type1` (built-in, 0 embedded bytes), form chrome
  - Helvetica — base-14 `Type1` (built-in, 0 embedded bytes), subtitle/footer
  - Arial Regular — `ttf` / `Type0`, 773,236 B, the field values
  - Arial Bold — `ttf` / `Type0`, 750,984 B, the overlapping-fragment glyphs
- **Span font names:** `Helvetica`, `Helvetica-Bold`, `ArialMT`, `Arial-BoldMT`
  (a base-14 family AND an embedded TrueType family on one page).
- **Dates:** `1/8/2026`, `10/11/1995`.
- **Overlapping-duplicate value (Field A):** the value `Cleared 4821` is stamped
  THREE times at hair-offset baselines (full + re-stamp + a "Cleared" fragment),
  so `document._merge_overlapping` collapses them into ONE editable box whose
  `Span.redact_bboxes` has length **3**. This is the box-recognition contract:
  editing the box redacts every member instead of leaving residue.
- **Empty area:** the band from ~y=410 to the footer is intentionally blank for
  the "Add Text" tool.
- **Subset:** no.
- **Span count (page 0):** 14 (one of which is the 3-member merged Field A box).

---

## Coverage matrix

| Requirement | Fixture |
| --- | --- |
| Multi-paragraph body, several real-sentence lines per paragraph | `body_paragraphs.pdf` |
| Multiple font sizes on one page | `multi_size.pdf` |
| Bold and italic runs | `bold_italic.pdf` |
| Colored (non-black) text | `colored_text.pdf` |
| Subsetted font (missing-glyph fallback) | `subset_font.pdf` |
| Two families on one page | `mixed_families.pdf` |
| Form layout (header bar, filled cells, overlapping-duplicate value, empty area for add-text) | `form_like.pdf` |

## Verification

Every fixture opens and renders to PNG without error (2x zoom, 1224x1584). The
PNGs (`*.png` next to each PDF) and the raw font report (`font_reports.json`)
are committed alongside. Spans also extract cleanly through the project's
`pdftexteditor.document.PDFDocument.spans()` layer with the correct PostScript
font names, sizes, colors, and baseline origins.

# Page & Document Management Fixtures

A second family of fixtures, built by **`build_pages_fixtures.py`** (not
`build_fixtures.py`), for the **page & document management** build: combine /
merge, split / separate, reorder, rotate, insert, duplicate, delete, open
multiple documents, and rotation handling. All are PII-FREE and built from
invented content. Each page carries a **big, distinct page label** plus a
colored header band and unique body copy, so a reorder (drag a thumbnail), a
rotate, or a delete is **verifiable by eye** in the rendered PNG. Fonts are the
same real macOS system faces embedded via `fontfile=`, so spans extract through
the editor's font tiers exactly like the single-page fixtures.

## How to rebuild

```sh
QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen \
  /Users/edward/Documents/GitHub/PDFTextEditor/.venv/bin/python \
  tests/fixtures/build_pages_fixtures.py
```

Each `*.pdf` is regenerated and a single verification `*.png` per file is
written with **every page stacked vertically** (1.5x zoom, 16 px gray gaps via
Pillow) so one image shows the whole document. The script then reopens each
fixture, prints `page_count` + per-page size / orientation / `/Rotate`, and
confirms it opens through `PDFDocument` and extracts spans on every page.

### 8. `three_page.pdf` — 3 pages, distinct labels, one landscape

The primary reorder / rotate / delete fixture. Three pages, each a different
color band, big label, and body, **one of them landscape** so mixed page sizes
are covered:

| Page | Size (pt) | Orientation | `/Rotate` | Label | Header band | Spans |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 0 | 612 × 792 | portrait | 0 | `PAGE ONE` | navy `(0.10, 0.16, 0.45)` | 7 |
| 1 | 792 × 612 | **landscape** | 0 | `PAGE TWO` | forest `(0.13, 0.45, 0.20)` | 7 |
| 2 | 612 × 792 | portrait | 0 | `PAGE THREE` | crimson `(0.79, 0.09, 0.13)` | 7 |

- **Labels** are 30 pt Arial Bold reversed white on the band; **section heads**
  are 20 pt Georgia Bold tinted to the band color; **body** is 12 pt Georgia
  (pages 0, 2) / Arial (page 1); each page also has a 10 pt Arial Italic slate
  footer naming its sheet number.
- **Distinct body per page** (library reading room / coastal survey / repair
  workshop) so after a reorder you can read which page moved where.
- **Mixed page size:** page 1 is landscape (`792 × 612`), pages 0 and 2 are
  portrait — exercises a thumbnail sidebar and page-op pipeline that must not
  assume uniform dimensions.
- **Page count:** 3.

### 9. `two_page.pdf` — 2 pages (DOC-B) for merge / combine testing

A **second document** to append onto `three_page.pdf` (or any other) when
testing combine / merge and appended-doc reordering. A teal/plum palette,
deliberately different from `three_page.pdf`'s navy/forest/crimson, so merged-in
pages are obviously from another source.

| Page | Size (pt) | Orientation | `/Rotate` | Label | Header band | Spans |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 0 | 612 × 792 | portrait | 0 | `DOC-B P1` | teal `(0.07, 0.45, 0.49)` | 7 |
| 1 | 612 × 792 | portrait | 0 | `DOC-B P2` | plum `(0.45, 0.16, 0.50)` | 7 |

- Same label / head / body / footer typography as `three_page.pdf` (Arial Bold
  labels, Georgia Bold heads, Arial body, Arial Italic footer) but a glossary /
  references theme so appended pages read as Appendix B.
- **Page count:** 2.

### 10. `rotated_doc.pdf` — 1 page with `/Rotate 90` (rotation handling)

A single page whose **`/Rotate` flag is 90**, to test that the editor maps
derotated rawdict text-space coordinates into the rotated display space (via
`PDFDocument.rotation_matrix`) before drawing the selection overlay / hit-testing.

- Content is laid out in ordinary upright text space; `page.set_rotation(90)` is
  then applied, so the **displayed** sheet is turned 90° clockwise. The
  underlying MediaBox stays `612 × 792` portrait; `page.rect` (and so the model)
  reports the **rotated** dimensions `792 × 612` because PyMuPDF accounts for
  `/Rotate`.
- A **plum band** runs down the text-space left edge; after the 90° display
  rotation it lands along the **top** of the rendered image, so the rotation
  reads at a glance.
- `PDFDocument.page_rotation(0)` returns **90**; `spans(0)` extracts **8** runs
  (the model sees derotated text-space coordinates, which `rotation_matrix` maps
  into display space).
- Big `ROTATED 90°` label (Arial Bold) + an explanatory body (Georgia).
- **Page count:** 1.

## Coverage matrix (page management)

| Requirement | Fixture |
| --- | --- |
| Multi-page document, distinct per-page labels for reorder/delete verification | `three_page.pdf` |
| Mixed page sizes (portrait + landscape) on one document | `three_page.pdf` (page 1 landscape) |
| Second document to append for combine / merge | `two_page.pdf` |
| Page with `/Rotate 90` for rotation handling | `rotated_doc.pdf` |

## Verification (page management)

Built and confirmed with the project venv:

- `three_page.pdf` — 3 pages: `[612×792 portrait rot0]`, `[792×612 LANDSCAPE
  rot0]`, `[612×792 portrait rot0]`; spans per page `[7, 7, 7]`.
- `two_page.pdf` — 2 pages: both `612×792 portrait rot0`; spans `[7, 7]`.
- `rotated_doc.pdf` — 1 page: `792×612` (rotated dims), `page_rotation` = **90**;
  spans `[8]`.

Each fixture opens through `pdftexteditor.document.PDFDocument`, reports the
expected `page_count`, sizes, and `/Rotate` values, and extracts spans on every
page. The stacked `*.png` for each is committed alongside for visual inspection.

<!-- REFLOW FIXTURES -->
## Reflow & continuous-scroll fixtures

Built by `build_reflow_fixtures.py` for the **smart paragraph grouping + auto
reflow** and **continuous vertical scroll** build. PII-free, invented content;
real system faces embedded via `fontfile=` (Arial for Helvetica, Times New
Roman), so spans extract through the editor's font tiers like every other fixture.

### `paragraphs.pdf` — the multi-line-paragraph bug, plus must-not-merge elements

One US-Letter page (612 × 792) holding, top to bottom:

- **Heading** — `Operations Planning Notice`, 20 pt Arial Bold, navy.
- **Target body paragraph** — ONE paragraph hand-wrapped to **5 lines** at
  **11.0 pt Times**, constant **15.0 pt leading**, left x =
  72. Grouping must merge these into **one** editable, reflowable box.
- **2-column label:value table** — 4 rows (bold label at x=72, value at
  x=250). Distinct fields: must NOT merge label↔value or row↔row.
- **Short 2-line note** — 10 pt Times Italic, slate, set apart by a wider gap.
- **Bulleted list** — 4 bullets (crimson `•` + 11 pt Times text). Each bullet is
  its own item; must NOT collapse into a paragraph.

**The bug, measured through `PDFDocument.spans(0)`:** the target paragraph is
currently **5 SEPARATE spans** (one per visual line), not one box. Total spans
on the page: **24** (19 of them the heading / table / note / bullets).
Grouping must collapse the five rows below into ONE box WITHOUT swallowing any of
the 19 surrounding spans.

Per-line spans of the target paragraph (proof they are separate; leading is the
baseline-to-baseline delta and is constant, the signature grouping keys on):

| Line | (blk,line,span) | bbox | origin | size | leading | text |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 0 | `(1,0,0)` | (72.0, 158.2, 378.09, 170.38) | (72.0, 168.0) | 11.0 | — | The quarterly operations review will convene i… |
| 1 | `(1,1,0)` | (72.0, 173.2, 396.52, 185.38) | (72.0, 183.0) | 11.0 | 15.0 | second Tuesday of the month, beginning promptl… |
| 2 | `(1,2,0)` | (72.0, 188.2, 387.26, 200.38) | (72.0, 198.0) | 11.0 | 15.0 | and every team lead is expected to bring a sho… |
| 3 | `(1,3,0)` | (72.0, 203.2, 399.79, 215.38) | (72.0, 213.0) | 11.0 | 15.0 | milestones reached since the previous session … |
| 4 | `(1,4,0)` | (72.0, 218.2, 393.98, 230.38) | (72.0, 228.0) | 11.0 | 15.0 | still need a decision from the wider group bef… |

### `multipage_body.pdf` — continuous scroll + cross-page find/replace

**4 pages**, two multi-line body paragraphs per page (11 pt Times, 15 pt
leading), each page labelled `Site Bulletin — Page N of 4`. For continuous
vertical scroll (lazy render of visible pages, fit-to-width, scrolling across
page boundaries). The phrase **`monitoring cadence`** appears **exactly once per page**
(4 total) so a document-wide find/replace has a known, page-spanning
hit count to verify.

| Page | Spans | `monitoring cadence` hits | Paragraph line counts |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 0 | 9 | 1 | [5, 3] |
| 1 | 9 | 1 | [5, 3] |
| 2 | 9 | 1 | [5, 3] |
| 3 | 9 | 1 | [5, 3] |

> **Whitespace note (important for grouping + find/replace):** PyMuPDF's
> `insert_text` maps the ASCII space glyph to **U+00A0 (NBSP)** in the text these
> fixtures extract via `get_text` / `rawdict` (verified on this system), and the
> soft hyphen in a word like `One-page` extracts as **U+00AD**. This is faithful
> to real PDFs, where extracted whitespace is often a space variant. Word-level
> grouping, reflow tokenization, and find/replace must therefore treat U+00A0 as
> a word separator (and normalize U+00AD) — a naive search for an ASCII-space
> phrase returns **zero** hits. The hit counts above are measured AFTER
> normalizing NBSP→space, so they reflect what a NBSP-aware find/replace sees.

### Verification

Both fixtures open through `pdftexteditor.document.PDFDocument`. `paragraphs.png`
and `multipage_body.png` (all four pages stacked) are written alongside for
visual inspection. The per-line report above is emitted by the builder so the
grouping engine can be validated: after grouping, `spans(0)`-equivalent box
enumeration should report the five paragraph rows as **one** box while leaving
the heading, the 4 table rows, the 2-line note, and the 4 bullets as
distinct boxes.

<!-- ANNOTATION FIXTURES -->
## Annotations & markup fixture

Built by `build_annot_fixture.py` for the **annotations & markup** build
(highlight/underline/strikeout/squiggly, sticky notes, existing-annot
management). PII-free, invented content with fake neutral names only
("Jordan Carter", "Acme Corp", "Riley Morgan"); real system faces embedded via
`fontfile=` like every other fixture.

### `annot_target.pdf` — 2 pages; page 2 ships pre-existing annotations

| Page | Content | Pre-existing annots |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 0 | navy Arial Bold heading, 5-line Georgia body paragraph, slate Arial footer | none (clean markup staging target) |
| 1 | navy Georgia Bold heading, 5-line Georgia body paragraph, slate Arial footer | 1 highlight (stroke `(1, 0.85, 0)`, over the word `milestone`) + 1 sticky note (content `Reviewed by Riley Morgan`, empty Title) |

- **Page 1 is annot-free** so markup staging tests start from a known-empty
  annotation census; known words for quad targeting include `Jordan`,
  `quarterly`, `Acme`, `markup`.
- **Page 2's file annots** exercise the existing-annot override map (delete /
  edit contents / note move): the highlight + note survive `insert_pdf` into
  the model's working copy (probe-verified), so `working[1].annots()` reports
  both on open.
- **Verification:** the builder reopens the file and asserts 2 pages, page-1
  annots == 0, page-2 annots == {highlight, text}, note content
  `Reviewed by Riley Morgan`; opens through `PDFDocument` (spans per page
  `[3, 3]`). `annot_target.png` (both pages stacked, 1.5x) is committed
  alongside for visual inspection.

### How to rebuild

```sh
QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen \
  /Users/edward/Documents/GitHub/PDFTextEditor/.venv/bin/python \
  tests/fixtures/build_annot_fixture.py
```

# Document Tools Fixture

Built by **`build_doctools_fixture.py`** (not `build_fixtures.py`) for the
**page & document tools** build: metadata/properties, text + image export, and
(later milestones) watermark/header-footer, crop, security, and optimize. PII-free,
invented content with fake neutral names only (Acme Corp, Jordan Carter, Riley
Morgan).

## How to rebuild

```sh
QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen \
  /Users/edward/Documents/GitHub/PDFTextEditor/.venv/bin/python \
  tests/fixtures/build_doctools_fixture.py
```

### 11. `doc_tools.pdf` — 3 letter pages WITH a real info dictionary

The metadata-carry fixture: the only committed PDF whose info dict is
populated, so the regression "every save silently strips Title/Author" is
testable against a real file.

- **Metadata:** title `Quarterly Review Agenda`, author `Jordan Carter`,
  subject `Acme Corp quarterly review`, keywords `agenda, review, acme`.
- **Pages:** 3 × US Letter portrait (612 × 792), navy header band + white
  Georgia Bold 22 pt heading per page, four 12 pt Georgia body lines spaced
  28 pt apart (past the paragraph-grouping first-gap ceiling, so each line is
  its own editable box), 10 pt slate footer. Spans per page: **[6, 6, 6]**.
- **Embedded faces:** Georgia Regular + Georgia Bold — `ttf` / `Type0`, full
  embeds (not subset).
- **Edit target:** the word `revenue` appears **exactly once** in the whole
  document (page 1 body), so edit-then-export assertions are unambiguous.
- **Compression:** saved with **`deflate=False`** (774,726 B raw), so the
  optimize milestone has a measurable before/after byte delta on this fixture.
- **Verification:** `doc_tools.png` (all three pages stacked, 1.5× zoom) is
  committed alongside; the builder re-opens the file through `PDFDocument`
  and asserts the metadata carry plus the single-`revenue` contract.

## Coverage matrix (document tools)

| Requirement | Fixture |
| --- | --- |
| Populated info dict for the metadata-carry regression | `doc_tools.pdf` |
| Uncompressed source for a measurable optimize delta | `doc_tools.pdf` |
| Single-occurrence edit target for export diff tests | `doc_tools.pdf` |
| Runtime-only fixtures (3-entry TOC carry; AES-256 encrypted copy) | built in `tempfile` by `tests/test_doctools.py`, never committed |

<!-- NAV FIXTURES -->
## Navigation fixture (bookmarks / outline)

Built by `build_nav_fixture.py` for the **navigation, menus & chrome**
workstream. PII-free, invented content (Acme Corp, Jordan Carter, Riley
Morgan); BASE-14 Helvetica only, so the fixture has zero font dependencies.

### `outline_doc.pdf` — 5 letter pages WITH a real outline

The bookmark fixture: the only committed PDF whose outline is populated, so
the regression "every save silently wipes the bookmarks" and the
outline()/set_outline()/BookmarkPanel build are testable against a real file.

- **Outline:** `[[1, 'Welcome', 1], [2, 'Agenda', 2], [1, 'Policies', 4]]` (one nested entry under "Welcome"; "Policies"
  targets page 4, so `delete_page(1)` remaps it and dangles "Agenda").
- **Pages:** 5 x US Letter portrait (612 x 792), navy header band + white
  Helvetica-Bold 22 pt heading per page, four 12 pt Helvetica body lines
  spaced 28 pt apart (past the paragraph-grouping first-gap ceiling, so each
  line is its own editable box), 10 pt slate footer.
- **Fonts:** base-14 `helv` / `hebo` only — no embedded files, no system
  font requirement.
- **Edit target:** the word `handbook` appears **exactly once** in the whole
  document (page 1 body), so the edit-then-save TOC regression is
  unambiguous.
- **Verification:** `outline_doc.png` (all five pages stacked, 1.5x zoom) is
  committed alongside; the builder re-opens the file through `PDFDocument`
  and asserts the outline carry plus the single-`handbook` contract.

```sh
QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen \
  /Users/edward/Documents/GitHub/PDFTextEditor/.venv/bin/python \
  tests/fixtures/build_nav_fixture.py
```
<!-- /NAV FIXTURES -->

<!-- ACROFORM FIXTURE -->
# AcroForm Fixture

Built by **`build_acroform_fixture.py`** for the **forms** build (detect /
fill / navigate / flatten). PII-free, invented content with fake neutral
names only (Jordan Carter, Riley Morgan, Acme Corp). Two US-Letter pages
carrying a REAL AcroForm (`doc.is_form_pdf == 6`,
a truthy field COUNT); `form_like.pdf` remains the FLAT no-widget
regression control.

### 12. `acroform.pdf` — 2 pages, 7 widgets (6 fields)

Page 0 carries the "Acme Corp Onboarding" heading (embedded Arial Bold, the
text-edit-coexistence target) plus labels and five fields; page 1 carries one
plain span and the cross-page tab-wrap target.

| Page | Type | Field name | Initial value | Rect (text space) | Flags | On-state |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 0 | Text | `employee_name` | '' | (220, 130, 480, 152) | 0 | — |
| 0 | Text | `notes` | '' | (220, 170, 480, 240) | 4096 | — |
| 0 | CheckBox | `ack_policy` | 'Off' | (220, 262, 236, 278) | 0 | Yes |
| 0 | ComboBox | `department` | 'People Ops' | (220, 300, 400, 322) | 131072 | — |
| 0 | RadioButton | `shift` | 'Off' | (220, 342, 236, 358) | 32768 | Day |
| 0 | RadioButton | `shift` | 'Off' | (300, 342, 316, 358) | 32768 | Night |
| 1 | Text | `manager_name` | '' | (220, 130, 480, 152) | 0 | — |

- The `shift` radio group is built from **raw xref objects** (parent
  `/FT/Btn /Ff 32768`, kid widgets with `/AS /Off` + per-kid on/off form
  XObjects) because `page.add_widget` rejects radio widgets in PyMuPDF 1.27;
  once built, `page.widgets()` enumerates both kids with `on_state()`
  Day / Night.
- `notes` is multiline (`PDF_TX_FIELD_IS_MULTILINE`, flags 4096);
  `department` is a combobox over People Ops / Finance / Engineering with
  initial value `People Ops`; `ack_policy`'s on-state is `Yes`.
- Field borders are page CONTENT (thin gray frames), not widget borders, so
  widget ink deltas isolate the filled values.
- **Verification:** the builder reopens the file and asserts the census
  above; `acroform.png` (both pages stacked, 1.5x) is committed alongside.

### How to rebuild

```sh
QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen \
  /Users/edward/Documents/GitHub/PDFTextEditor/.venv/bin/python \
  tests/fixtures/build_acroform_fixture.py
```

<!-- /ACROFORM FIXTURE -->

<!-- IMAGE FIXTURES BEGIN -->
## Existing-images fixture (images & signatures M3)

Built by **`build_image_fixtures.py`**. PII-free, invented content with fake
neutral names only (Jordan Carter, Acme Corp, Riley Morgan).

### `image_doc.pdf` — one RGBA logo xref shared by two pages

The existing-image management fixture: detect / page-scoped delete / move of
images already in the file.

- **Pages:** 2 × US Letter portrait (612 × 792), embedded Arial/Georgia text
  (spans per page [3, 3]).
- **Logo:** ONE procedural 120 × 80 RGBA gradient (red→blue across, the
  bottom quarter fully transparent, so the embedded image carries an
  **SMask**). The SAME xref is placed on page 1 at
  `(392.0, 96.0, 512.0, 176.0)` and on page 2 at `(72.0, 96.0, 162.0, 156.0)` (both inserted
  with `keep_proportion=False`, so `get_image_info` round-trips the authored
  rects exactly).
- **Contract:** deleting the page-1 occurrence (scoped image-REMOVE
  redaction) must spare the page-1 text and the page-2 occurrence; the move
  macro must preserve transparency through `extract_image_bytes`.
- **Verification:** `image_doc.png` (both pages stacked, 1.5×) is committed
  alongside; the builder re-opens the file through `PDFDocument` and asserts
  the shared xref + authored rects + SMask.

### How to rebuild

```sh
QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen \
  /Users/edward/Documents/GitHub/PDFTextEditor/.venv/bin/python \
  tests/fixtures/build_image_fixtures.py
```
<!-- IMAGE FIXTURES END -->
